evidence_quotes.quote: stop rendering games and other interactive works of art unplayable after purchase.
I bought the game The Crew, and enjoyed it for many years.
However, the company that made it, Ubisoft, has ended support for the game, despite selling it as a product to me.
Now I can't play the game that I own anymore!

paragraphs.text: Hi! My name is Steven Velema, I'm a citizen of the Netherlands. I am writing to you on behalf of a movement that aims to stop rendering games and other interactive works of art unplayable after purchase. I have personal experience with this as I bought the game The Crew, and enjoyed it for many years. However, the company that made it, Ubisoft, has ended support for the game, despite selling it as a product to me. Now I can't play the game that I own anymore! I believe this goes against preservation, consumer rights and just plain common sense: I bought the game, it shouldn't explode right in front of me because the company that sold it to me pushed a button. I believe this fits in very well with the Digital Fairness Act and would be a fantastic addition to protect consumers from these malicious practices. Thank you for your time and consideration!
